title: [PROJECT] Waitlist Infrastructure & Notifications
description: Build production-ready waitlist system with Supabase backend, email confirmations, and real-time notifications

title: Initialize Supabase project and create waitlist database schema
description: 1. Create Supabase project at supabase.com named 'arcticruff-prod'. 2. In SQL Editor, execute: `CREATE TABLE waitlist (id UUID DEFAULT gen_random_uuid() PRIMARY KEY, email TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL, name TEXT NOT NULL, jacket_model TEXT NOT NULL, fur_color_preference TEXT NOT NULL, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW());` 3. Enable RLS: `ALTER TABLE waitlist ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;` 4. Create public insert policy: `CREATE POLICY "Allow public inserts" ON waitlist FOR INSERT WITH CHECK (true);` 5. Copy project URL and anon key from Project Settings > API. 6. Create `/Users/michaelguo/arcticruff/.env.local` with: `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL=https://[project-ref].supabase.co` and `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY=[anon-key]`. 7. Add `.env*.local` to `.gitignore` if not present. 8. For Vercel deployment, add these same env vars in Project Settings > Environment Variables.

title: Implement /api/waitlist POST endpoint and connect WaitlistForm
description: 1. Run `npm install @supabase/supabase-js`. 2. Create `/Users/michaelguo/arcticruff/src/lib/supabase.ts` with Supabase client: `import { createClient } from '@supabase/supabase-js'; export const supabase = createClient(process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL!, process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY!);` 3. Create `/Users/michaelguo/arcticruff/src/app/api/waitlist/route.ts` with POST handler: validate request body (email regex `/^[^\s@]+@[^\s@]+\.[^\s@]+$/`, required fields: email, name, jacket_model, fur_color_preference), insert via `supabase.from('waitlist').insert({...})`, return `NextResponse.json()` with 201 on success or 400/500 on error. 4. Update `/Users/michaelguo/arcticruff/src/components/WaitlistForm.tsx`: replace `setTimeout` mock (lines 69-71) with `const res = await fetch('/api/waitlist', { method: 'POST', headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' }, body: JSON.stringify({ email, name: firstName, jacket_model: model, fur_color_preference: variant }) });` Handle response: `if (res.ok) { setFormState('success'); } else { setFormState('error'); setErrorMsg('Something went wrong. Please try again.'); }` Add catch block for network errors.

title: Set up Resend email service and send branded confirmation emails
description: 1. Sign up at resend.com, verify domain `arcticruff.com` (or use `onboarding@resend.dev` for testing). 2. Generate API key from API Keys section. 3. Add `RESEND_API_KEY=[key]` to `.env.local` and Vercel env vars. 4. Run `npm install resend react-email @react-email/components`. 5. Create `/Users/michaelguo/arcticruff/emails/WaitlistConfirmation.tsx` using React Email components (Html, Head, Body, Container, Section, Text, Img, Button). Include: ArcticRuff logo (hosted image URL), gold accent color (#D4AF37), personalized greeting with `{name}`, selected jacket model and fur color, "What happens next" section (confirmation, updates, reservation details), footer with unsubscribe link. 6. Update `/Users/michaelguo/arcticruff/src/app/api/waitlist/route.ts`: import Resend, after successful Supabase insert, send email: `const resend = new Resend(process.env.RESEND_API_KEY); await resend.emails.send({ from: 'Arctic Ruff <hello@arcticruff.com>', to: email, subject: 'Welcome to the Arctic Ruff Waitlist', react: WaitlistConfirmation({ name, jacketModel, furColor }) });` Catch and log email errors but don't fail the request (email is best-effort).

title: Implement real-time webhook notifications for waitlist signups
[PROJECT] Admin Dashboard & Data Management
description: Internal admin interface to view, filter, export waitlist data and track key metrics

title: Implement admin authentication with Next.js middleware and session cookies
description: FILES: Create `app/admin/page.tsx`, `app/admin/layout.tsx`, `app/api/admin/login/route.ts`, `app/api/admin/logout/route.ts`, `middleware.ts`. Add `ADMIN_PASSWORD` to `.env.local`. IMPLEMENTATION: (1) Create POST `/api/admin/login` that compares body.password against process.env.ADMIN_PASSWORD, sets httpOnly cookie `admin_session=true` (secure, sameSite: 'lax', maxAge: 24h) on success. (2) Create POST `/api/admin/logout` that clears the cookie. (3) In `middleware.ts`, add matcher for `/admin/:path*`, check for `admin_session` cookie, redirect to `/admin/login` if missing. (4) Create `/app/admin/login/page.tsx` with password input form that POSTs to `/api/admin/login`. (5) Create `/app/admin/layout.tsx` with 'Admin Dashboard' header, 'Logout' button (calls `/api/admin/logout`), link to home. Style with Tailwind matching main site (same font, colors from globals.css). DONE: Can access /admin only after entering correct password, logout clears session, failed login shows error message.

title: Build server-side waitlist API and client-side table with TanStack Table
description: DEPENDENCIES: `npm install @tanstack/react-table`. FILES: Create `app/api/admin/waitlist/route.ts`, update `app/admin/page.tsx`, create `components/admin/WaitlistTable.tsx`. IMPLEMENTATION: (1) In `route.ts` GET handler, verify `admin_session` cookie (middleware already protects, but double-check). Use `@supabase/supabase-js` client to query `waitlist` table: `select('*').order('created_at', { ascending: false })`. Return JSON array. (2) In `WaitlistTable.tsx`, define columns: id (hidden), name, email, jacket_model, fur_color_preference, created_at (formatted with `new Date().toLocaleString()`). Use `useReactTable` hook with `getCoreRowModel`, `getSortedRowModel`, `getFilteredRowModel`, `getPaginationRowModel`. (3) Add controlled input for global search (filters across name + email using `setGlobalFilter`). Add select dropdowns for jacket_model and fur_color (use `column.setFilterValue`). (4) Render table with Tailwind styling: striped rows, sortable headers (click to toggle asc/desc), pagination controls (10/25/50 per page). (5) Fetch data via `fetch('/api/admin/waitlist')` in useEffect, display loading state. DONE: Table displays all waitlist entries, can search by name/email, filter by jacket model and fur color independently, sort by any column, paginate through results.

title: Add client-side CSV export button with filtered data
description: DEPENDENCIES: `npm install papaparse @types/papaparse`. FILES: Update `app/admin/page.tsx` and `components/admin/WaitlistTable.tsx`. IMPLEMENTATION: (1) In `WaitlistTable.tsx`, add 'Export to CSV' button in table header (Tailwind styled, same blue as CTA buttons). (2) On click, get current table instance's filtered rows via `table.getFilteredRowModel().rows`. Map rows to objects: `{ Name: row.original.name, Email: row.original.email, 'Jacket Model': row.original.jacket_model, 'Fur Color': row.original.fur_color_preference, 'Signup Date': new Date(row.original.created_at).toLocaleString() }`. (3) Use `papaparse.unparse()` to convert array to CSV string. (4) Create Blob with type 'text/csv', generate object URL, create temporary anchor element with download attribute = `arcticruff-waitlist-${new Date().toISOString().split('T')[0]}.csv`, programmatically click, revoke URL. DONE: Button exports currently filtered/searched data (respects active filters and search, ignores pagination), downloads as CSV with formatted columns, filename includes current date.

title: Add real-time stats panel with Supabase aggregations
description: FILES: Create `app/api/admin/stats/route.ts`, create `components/admin/StatsPanel.tsx`, update `app/admin/page.tsx`. IMPLEMENTATION: (1) In `route.ts` GET handler, run parallel Supabase queries: (a) Total count: `count('*')`, (b) Today count: `count('*').gte('created_at', new Date().setHours(0,0,0,0).toISOString())`, (c) Group by jacket_model: `select('jacket_model').count()`, (d) Group by fur_color_preference: `select('fur_color_preference').count()`. Find most popular by max count. Return JSON: `{ total: number, today: number, popularModel: string, popularColor: string }`. (2) In `StatsPanel.tsx`, fetch `/api/admin/stats` on mount. Display 4 cards in grid (2x2 on desktop, 1 col on mobile). Each card: icon (📊 total, 📅 today, 🧥 model, 🎨 color), label, value (large font). Use Tailwind: white bg, shadow, rounded, p-6. Handle loading state (skeleton). (3) Place `<StatsPanel />` at top of `/app/admin/page.tsx` above table. DONE: Stats panel shows correct counts (verify by comparing with table data), updates on page load, displays 'N/A' if no data, responsive layout.

title: [PROJECT] Stripe Checkout & Payment Processing
description: Enable direct jacket purchases with Stripe-powered checkout flow and order management

title: Initialize Stripe SDK and environment configuration
description:
1. Create Stripe account at stripe.com, enable test mode, copy Publishable Key and Secret Key from dashboard.
2. Run: `npm install @stripe/stripe-js stripe`
3. Create `.env.local` in project root with:
   NEXT_PUBLIC_STRIPE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY=pk_test_...
   STRIPE_SECRET_KEY=sk_test_...
   NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL=https://[project].supabase.co
   NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY=eyJh...
   SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY=eyJh...
   RESEND_API_KEY=re_...
4. Create `/lib/stripe.ts`:
   - Export server-side Stripe instance: `new Stripe(process.env.STRIPE_SECRET_KEY!, { apiVersion: '2024-11-20.acacia' })`
   - Export client-side loader: `export const getStripe = () => loadStripe(process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_STRIPE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY!)`
5. Add `.env.local` to `.gitignore` (verify exists).

Acceptance: Can import `{ stripe }` from `/lib/stripe` server-side and `getStripe()` client-side without errors.

title: Build checkout page with product configuration and Stripe Payment Element
description:
1. Create `/lib/products.ts`:
   - Export `JACKET_MODELS` array (same as WaitlistForm)
   - Export `FUR_VARIANTS` array (natural, shadow, ivory)
   - Export `SIZES` array: ['XS', 'S', 'M', 'L', 'XL', 'XXL']
   - Export pricing function:
     ```typescript
     export function calculatePrice(furColor: string): number {
       const basePriceCents = 89900; // $899 CAD
       const premiumFurs = ['shadow', 'ivory'];
       return premiumFurs.includes(furColor) ? basePriceCents + 15000 : basePriceCents;
     }
     ```
2. Create `/app/checkout/page.tsx` ('use client'):
   - Product selection form: jacket model dropdown, fur color radio buttons, size dropdown, quantity number input (default 1, max 5)
   - Display calculated price: `const price = calculatePrice(furColor) * quantity` formatted as CAD dollars
   - Integrate Stripe Elements:
     ```tsx
     import { Elements } from '@stripe/react-stripe-js';
     import { getStripe } from '@/lib/stripe';
     const stripePromise = getStripe();
     <Elements stripe={stripePromise} options={{ clientSecret }}>
       <CheckoutFormComponent />
     </Elements>
     ```
   - On mount, call `/api/create-payment-intent` with selection, receive `client_secret`
   - Render PaymentElement from `@stripe/react-stripe-js`
   - Pre-fill customer email/name from URL params: `?email=...&name=...`
3. Style matching ArcticRuff: dark background (#0D0D0D), gold (#C8A882) CTAs, luxury typography

Acceptance: /checkout displays product selector, price updates dynamically, Stripe payment form renders without console errors.

title: Complete Stripe payment flow with order storage and success page
description:
1. Create `/app/api/create-payment-intent/route.ts` POST handler:
   - Accept JSON: `{ jacketModel, furColor, size, quantity, email, name }`
   - Validate inputs, calculate amount: `calculatePrice(furColor) * quantity`
   - Create PaymentIntent:
     ```typescript
     const paymentIntent = await stripe.paymentIntents.create({
       amount: totalCents,
       currency: 'cad',
       metadata: { jacketModel, furColor, size, quantity, email, name },
       receipt_email: email
     });
     return NextResponse.json({ clientSecret: paymentIntent.client_secret });
     ```
2. Update `/app/checkout/page.tsx`:
   - On form submit, call `stripe.confirmPayment({ elements, confirmParams: { return_url: `${window.location.origin}/checkout/success` } })`
   - Handle errors, display via toast or error message
3. Create `/app/checkout/success/page.tsx` ('use client'):
   - Extract `payment_intent` from URL query: `const searchParams = useSearchParams(); const piId = searchParams.get('payment_intent');`
   - Fetch PaymentIntent metadata from `/api/orders/confirm?payment_intent=${piId}`
   - Display: Order number (last 8 chars of PI ID), customer email, jacket details, "Receipt sent to your email"
   - "What's next" timeline: Order confirmed → In production (6 weeks) → Shipped → Delivered
4. Create `/app/api/orders/confirm/route.ts` GET handler:
   - Retrieve PaymentIntent by ID: `const pi = await stripe.paymentIntents.retrieve(piId)`
   - If successful, insert into Supabase `orders` table:
     ```sql
     CREATE TABLE orders (
       id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
       payment_intent_id TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL,
       email TEXT NOT NULL,
       customer_name TEXT,
       jacket_model TEXT,
       fur_color TEXT,
       size TEXT,
       quantity INT,
       amount_cents INT,
       created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
     );
     ```
   - Return order details as JSON
5. Add webhook handler at `/app/api/webhooks/stripe/route.ts` for `payment_intent.succeeded` to store orders asynchronously (optional but recommended).

Acceptance: Successful test payment (use Stripe test card 4242 4242 4242 4242) redirects to /checkout/success showing order details. Order appears in Supabase `orders` table.

title: [PROJECT] SEO & Analytics Setup
description: Optimize for search engines and implement tracking to measure conversion funnel performance

title: Implement Next.js metadata API with OpenGraph and Twitter Cards
description:
DEPENDENCIES: None (use Next.js built-in metadata API)

FILES TO MODIFY:
- /app/layout.tsx: Update the root metadata export with complete SEO configuration:
  - metadata.title: 'ArcticRuff - Luxury Winter Jackets with Premium Fur Trim'
  - metadata.description: 'Experience unparalleled warmth with ArcticRuff luxury winter jackets. Premium Canadian-made outerwear featuring ethically-sourced fur trim in Alpine White and Midnight Fox.' (158 chars)
  - metadata.openGraph: { title, description, images: [{ url: '/og-image.png', width: 1200, height: 630 }], type: 'website', locale: 'en_CA', siteName: 'ArcticRuff' }
  - metadata.twitter: { card: 'summary_large_image', title, description, images: ['/og-image.png'] }
  - metadata.keywords: ['luxury winter jackets', 'premium outerwear', 'fur trim jackets', 'Canadian winter coats', 'ArcticRuff']
  - metadata.authors: [{ name: 'ArcticRuff' }]
  - metadata.robots: 'index, follow'

FILES TO CREATE:
- /app/icon.png (32x32 or 192x192): Square logo for favicon (Next.js auto-generates favicon.ico)
- /app/apple-icon.png (180x180): Apple touch icon
- /app/opengraph-image.png (1200x630): Hero product shot on white background with 'ArcticRuff' branding overlay

IMPLEMENTATION:
1. Export metadata object from layout.tsx (Next.js will auto-inject meta tags)
2. Create image assets in /app directory (Next.js convention-based metadata files)
3. Verify output by viewing page source at https://arcticruff-deploy.vercel.app

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- OpenGraph Debugger (https://www.opengraph.xyz/) shows correct image, title, description
- Twitter Card Validator shows large image card preview
- Browser tab shows favicon and apple-icon on iOS devices
- View page source contains: <meta property="og:image" content=".../og-image.png" />

title: Add schema.org Product JSON-LD with aggregate rating and offers
description:
DEPENDENCIES: None

FILES TO CREATE:
- /components/StructuredData.tsx:
  - Accept props: { name: string, description: string, image: string, price: number, currency: string, model: string }
  - Return <Script id="product-jsonld" type="application/ld+json" strategy="afterInteractive"> with JSON.stringify() of schema.org/Product structure
  - Include fields: @context: 'https://schema.org', @type: 'Product', name, description, brand: { @type: 'Brand', name: 'ArcticRuff' }, offers: { @type: 'Offer', price, priceCurrency: 'CAD', availability: 'https://schema.org/PreOrder', url: 'https://arcticruff-deploy.vercel.app/checkout' }, aggregateRating: { @type: 'AggregateRating', ratingValue: '4.9', reviewCount: '127' }, image: [image]

FILES TO MODIFY:
- /app/page.tsx (main product page): Import StructuredData component and render with props for each jacket model (Denali Summit, Yukon Explorer, Baffin Ridge). Pass heroImage URL, base price, model name.

IMPLEMENTATION:
1. Use next/script for Script component (ensures proper loading timing)
2. Create reusable component accepting product props
3. Render one StructuredData instance per product variant on page
4. Test JSON syntax validity before deployment

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Google Rich Results Test (https://search.google.com/test/rich-results) validates Product markup with zero errors
- JSON-LD visible in page source within <script type="application/ld+json">
- Google Search Console (after indexing) shows Product rich result eligibility
- Structured data includes all required fields: name, image, offers.price, offers.priceCurrency, offers.availability

title: Configure Next.js dynamic sitemap and robots.txt for crawlers
description:
DEPENDENCIES: None (Next.js built-in sitemap generation)

FILES TO CREATE:
- /app/sitemap.ts: Export default async function sitemap(): Promise<MetadataRoute.Sitemap>
  - Return array of URL objects:
    - { url: 'https://arcticruff-deploy.vercel.app', lastModified: new Date(), changeFrequency: 'weekly', priority: 1.0 }
    - { url: 'https://arcticruff-deploy.vercel.app/checkout', lastModified: new Date(), changeFrequency: 'monthly', priority: 0.8 }
    - For each model in ['denali-summit', 'yukon-explorer', 'baffin-ridge']: { url: `https://arcticruff-deploy.vercel.app/products/${model}`, lastModified: new Date(), changeFrequency: 'monthly', priority: 0.7 }
  - Import MetadataRoute from 'next'

- /app/robots.ts: Export default function robots(): MetadataRoute.Robots
  - Return { rules: { userAgent: '*', allow: '/', disallow: ['/admin/', '/api/'] }, sitemap: 'https://arcticruff-deploy.vercel.app/sitemap.xml' }
  - Import MetadataRoute from 'next'

IMPLEMENTATION:
1. Next.js automatically serves /sitemap.xml and /robots.txt from these route handlers
2. Deploy to Vercel (no manual file placement in /public needed)
3. Verify URLs are accessible after deployment

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- https://arcticruff-deploy.vercel.app/sitemap.xml returns valid XML with all URLs
- https://arcticruff-deploy.vercel.app/robots.txt returns valid robots.txt with sitemap reference
- XML Sitemap Validator (https://www.xml-sitemaps.com/validate-xml-sitemap.html) shows zero errors
- Google Search Console sitemap submission succeeds with all URLs discovered

title: Install GA4 with GTM-style event tracking for waitlist and checkout funnels
description:
DEPENDENCIES: npm install react-ga4

FILES TO CREATE:
- /lib/analytics.ts:
  - Import ReactGA from 'react-ga4'
  - Export initGA(): void => ReactGA.initialize('G-XXXXXXXXXX', { gaOptions: { anonymizeIp: true } })
  - Export trackEvent(eventName: string, params: Record<string, any>): void => ReactGA.event(eventName, params)
  - Export trackPageView(path: string): void => ReactGA.send({ hitType: 'pageview', page: path })

FILES TO MODIFY:
- /app/layout.tsx: Add GA4 initialization in useEffect (client component wrapper):
  - Create client component ClientAnalytics.tsx: 'use client'; useEffect(() => { if (typeof window !== 'undefined') initGA(); }, [])
  - Import and render <ClientAnalytics /> in layout.tsx body

- /app/api/waitlist/route.ts (or wherever WaitlistForm.tsx submits):
  - After successful DB insert, call: trackEvent('waitlist_signup', { model: body.jacketModel, fur_color: body.furColor, timestamp: new Date().toISOString() })

- /app/checkout/page.tsx:
  - On component mount: trackEvent('checkout_started', { value: selectedProduct.price, currency: 'CAD', items: [selectedProduct.model] })
  - After Stripe payment success: trackEvent('purchase', { transaction_id: paymentIntent.id, value: amount, currency: 'CAD', items: [{ item_id: model, item_name: name, price: price }] })

ENVIRONMENT SETUP:
1. Create GA4 property at https://analytics.google.com/
2. Get Measurement ID (G-XXXXXXXXXX) from Admin > Data Streams > Web
3. Store in .env.local: NEXT_PUBLIC_GA_MEASUREMENT_ID=G-XXXXXXXXXX
4. Update analytics.ts to use process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_GA_MEASUREMENT_ID

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- GA4 Realtime view shows active users when visiting site
- GA4 DebugView (enable via Chrome extension) shows 'waitlist_signup' event with correct parameters
- Events appear in GA4 Events report within 24 hours
- Conversion funnel in GA4 Explorations shows: page_view → waitlist_signup → checkout_started → purchase
- Test event tracking by submitting waitlist form and verifying in DebugView
